steps.title: Identify the boundary between two independent clauses
steps.content: After the blank, the text contains two independent clauses: “the hole's small size restricts light to a single ray” and “all light passing through the hole can only arrive at a single destination.” These must be properly separated or connected.

steps.title: Apply the correct sentence boundary rule
steps.content:
Option B uses a period to create a clear sentence break, then starts a new sentence with a dependent clause (Because the hole's small size restricts light to a single ray) followed by a comma and an independent clause. A period marks a sentence boundary, and a dependent clause followed by a comma and an independent clause is standard, correct structure.

Options A, C, and D fail to properly separate the clauses: A leaves them ambiguously connected in one sentence, C creates a comma splice, and D ambiguously connects them after "because."

That matches choice B — B is correct.
